free_text: On the possibility of using deuteron-disintegration
reactions to investigate nuclear surfaces
Eng.trans.of J,IZV,34,1714,1970
#NSR 1970NE16
Eng.trans.of J,UFZ,16,401,1971.
Russian translation is also available (#NSR 1971NE07).
Isochronous cyclotron U-120
No information on sources of uncertainties
Neutron and proton were detected in coincidence
The natural targets were prepared by rolling metal
foils to the required thickness. The isotopic targets
were obtained by electrolytic deposition from enriched
specimens and annealing for two hours at 800 degrees
centigrade in vacuum to reduce the hydrogen content.
Protons were recorded by a  stilbene crystal of
diameter 10 mm, height 1.2 mm and by a photomultiplier
FEU-13. The neutron detector (also a stilbene crystal
of diameter 50 mm and height 50 mm and  an FEU-13 was
shifted to the plane of the incident beam and the
proton detector was placed at a distance of 70 cm from
the target.
The cross section were normalized with an error of
+-5% for 58Ni and 64Ni and +-10% for 62Ni and 63Cu. The
disintegration cross sections for 60Ni relative to
natural copper were normalized with an error of +-10%.
UKRNDC
